name: Eekso — The Broken Foyer
dmNotes: ENTRY SCENE. Thesis of the dungeon: many owners, none recent — and one god left standing on purpose. Don't explain Torm; let them look. The rugs are pit traps down to the Pitfall Prison; falling is the intended alternate entrance, not a punishment. If someone drops, switch to 'The Pitfall Prison' and run them split — that's a feature.

music.label: Something's off
music.file: music/Russell Shaw - What Lies Behind- Open the Door.mp3

scriptText:
# The Broken Foyer

- Dry, still, cold air. Dust *hanging* — the kind that only hangs where nobody walks

- A hall built to impress, half knocked down, patched with cheaper stone by a worse hand — then abandoned again

- Many owners. None recent.

## The Four Gods

{{music:curious}}

Four pedestals stand along the far wall, and on three of them there is nothing left worth naming — a pair of feet, a head lying twenty feet away face-down, powder and a hand.

The fourth is *perfect*. He stands with his sword point-down and both palms on the pommel, head bowed. And there is no dust on his shoulders. Somebody has been coming here, for a long time, to wipe this one statue down.

> [!dm] **Torm, god of self-sacrifice.** Religion DC 10 to name him; any faith background knows him cold.

## The Rugs

{{music:unease}}

- Good rugs, layered thick, running toward the doors — laid like a runner for a guest

> [!dm] **They are pit traps** — open shafts down to the Pitfall Prison, spiked at the bottom.
>
> - **Spot:** Investigation DC 15 — the rugs *sag*, they don't lie flat. Actively prodding the floor finds them automatically.
> - **Step on:** DC 13 Dex to catch the lip and go prone at the edge
> - **The drop:** 30 ft — **3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing** from the spikes (DC 13 Dex halves the spike damage only) {{music:drop}}
> - A dropped PC is **impaled** — switch to **'Eekso — The Pitfall Prison'** and read *The Landing*. Run them split. This is a legitimate way in, not a mistake.

## The Old Camp

- Bedrolls still laid out; a cold ring of stones, ash gone to grey powder

- Packs cut open and rifled — **from the outside, after they were left**

- A tally scratched by the bedrolls, stopped mid-count

## East — The Dining Hall

- Long tables shoved aside or broken down for firewood never burned

- Stools stacked neatly in a corner by someone tidy, decades ago

- Dry cups, dry bowls, one rot-black smear

ideas.text: Someone wipes Torm's statue down regularly — no dust on the shoulders, fresh finger-tracks in the grime
Every footprint in the dust goes deeper in. Nothing has ever walked back out.
The tally by the bedrolls stops mid-count — the last mark is a partial stroke
The rugs are laid like a runner leading to the doors — the trap dressed as hospitality

entities.name: Statue of Torm (intact, tended)
entities.note: God of self-sacrifice. Three siblings destroyed; this one dusted regularly. The necromancer's signature.

entities.name: The rug pit traps
entities.note: Investigation DC 15 / Dex DC 13. 30 ft: 3d6 bludgeoning + 2d10 piercing, impaled on landing.

entities.name: The abandoned camp
entities.note: Left in a hurry; packs rifled from outside afterward.
